_metadata.note: One count per coded passage per speech. A passage receiving two stacked codes counts once per code. D2 codes are not applied to speeches in v3 — they belong to the formal documentation corpus (Corpus B). Apertura sessions (142, 143, 144) added in second coding pass on 2026-06-08.

apertura_analytical_observations.climate_content_evolution: Explicit climate-adjacent content increases significantly across the three apertura speeches: the 2024 speech has no direct climate references (codes capture foundational ideological framework only); the 2025 speech has limited climate adjacency (deregulation scale, Mercosur rejection, woke bundling); the 2026 speech contains the corpus's most explicit environmentalism dismissal ('prejuicios ambientalistas absurdos') and the most direct rejection of the multilateral governance era. This evolution is analytically significant: as Milei's government consolidates, the implicit anti-climate framework becomes explicit climate obstruction rhetoric.
apertura_analytical_observations.apertura_vs_international_forum: Average codes per apertura speech: 6.7. Average codes per international forum speech (Davos/UN, 6 speeches): 11.2. International forums remain the primary venue for explicit climate obstruction discourse, likely because they provide the multilateral audience against which Milei most sharply defines his position. Apertura speeches construct the domestic ideological infrastructure.
apertura_analytical_observations.Temporal_Appropriation_threshold: Temporal_Appropriation now confirmed in 4 corpus instances (Davos 2024 × 1, UN 2025 × 1, Davos 2025 × 1 [inferred], Apertura 144 × 1). Four instances across the full corpus exceeds the formalization threshold of 2+ appearances. Researcher should consider promoting to permanent deductive sub-code in next codebook revision.
key_new_passages: 'con cuidados, pero lejos de prejuicios ambientalistas absurdos' (Apertura 144) — strongest explicit anti-environmentalism passage in apertura corpus
'La era de la cooperación global sin brújula moral ha terminado' (Apertura 144) — most direct rejection of multilateral governance era
'la moral occidental como política de estado' para los próximos 50 años (Apertura 144) — attempts permanent normative closure against future climate governance mandates
'La motosierra no es solo un programa de gobierno, es una política de estado' (Apertura 143) — permanence claim for deregulatory project
Elon Musk/DOGE alignment as transnational validation (Apertura 143) — globalizes deregulatory revolution narrative
apertura_analytical_observations.dominant_mechanism_confirmed: D1.1 increases from 55% to 59% of total codes with apertura speeches added, further confirming that state/governance delegitimization is the primary rhetorical mechanism across all speech venues — not just international forums. The apertura speeches contribute proportionally more D1.1 codes (15 new D1.1 of 20 total new codes = 75%) than the previous corpus ratio (55%), reflecting the domestic governance focus of state-of-nation addresses.
apertura_analytical_observations.no_D2_in_speeches_confirmed: Zero D2 codes in v3 full corpus (9 speeches). All institutional announcements and governance changes referenced in speeches coded as D1.
